title: Leymus × vancouverensis
taxonomy: Leymus × vancouverensis is a hybrid of two Leymus species, Leymus mollis and Leymus triticoides. Leymus × vancouverensis is a perennial grass native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. Its classification is a combination of its parent species, making it a natural hybrid. Leymus × vancouverensis is often used in landscaping and restoration projects due to its hardiness and attractive foliage. This hybrid is part of the Poaceae family, which includes many other types of grasses.

intro: Leymus × vancouverensis, also known as Vancouver grass or wheat grass, is a hybrid grass species that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress and improving overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Leymus × vancouverensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other polyphenolic compounds that are responsible for its medicinal properties.
